main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Client Main Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct client_session_state_t
Client connection session state for session_client_like integration.

Functions


int client_main (void)
Client mode entry point for unified binary.

Variables


thread_pool_t * g_client_worker_pool = NULL
Global client worker thread pool. app_client_t * g_client = NULL
Global client application context. struct webrtc_peer_manager * g_peer_manager = NULL
Global WebRTC peer manager (legacy compatibility).

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Main Entry Point

This module serves as the main entry point for the ascii-chat client application. It orchestrates the entire client lifecycle including initialization, connection management, and the primary event loop that manages reconnection logic.

Architecture Overview

The client follows a modular threading architecture:

  • Main thread: Connection management and event coordination
  • Data reception thread: Handles incoming packets from server
  • Ping thread: Maintains connection keepalive
  • Webcam capture thread: Captures and transmits video frames
  • Audio capture thread: Captures and transmits audio data (optional)

Connection Management

The client implements robust reconnection logic with exponential backoff:

1.
Initial connection attempt
2.
On connection loss, attempt reconnection with increasing delays
3.
Maximum delay cap to prevent excessive wait times
4.
Clean thread lifecycle management across reconnections

Thread Lifecycle

Each connection cycle follows this pattern:

1.
Connection Establishment: Socket creation and server handshake
2.
Thread Spawning: Start all worker threads for the connection
3.
Active Monitoring: Monitor connection health and thread status
4.
Connection Loss Detection: Detect broken connections via thread exit
5.
Cleanup Phase: Join all threads and reset connection state
6.
Reconnection Cycle: Repeat from step 1 unless shutdown requested

Integration Points

  • server.c: Connection establishment and socket management
  • protocol.c: Initial capability negotiation and join packets
  • display.c: Terminal initialization and control
  • capture.c: Media capture subsystem initialization
  • audio.c: Audio subsystem initialization (if enabled)
  • keepalive.c: Ping thread management

Error Handling

The main loop implements graceful error recovery:

  • Fatal initialization errors cause immediate exit
  • Network errors trigger reconnection attempts
  • Signal handling for graceful shutdown (SIGINT, SIGWINCH)
  • Resource cleanup on all exit paths

Platform Compatibility

Uses platform abstraction layer for:

  • Socket operations and network initialization
  • Thread management and synchronization
  • Signal handling differences between Unix and Windows
  • Terminal I/O and control sequences

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Version

2.0

Definition in file client/main.c.

Function Documentation

int client_main (void )

Client mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete client lifecycle including:

  • Client-specific subsystem initialization (display, capture, audio)
  • Server connection establishment with exponential backoff reconnection
  • Main event loop coordinating connection state and thread lifecycle
  • Media streaming (webcam frames and optional audio)
  • Frame display to terminal
  • Graceful shutdown and resource cleanup

Lifecycle Overview

The client follows this high-level state flow:

1.
Initialize: Set up display, capture, audio subsystems
2.
Connect Loop:
•
Establish TCP connection to server
•
Perform cryptographic handshake
•
Spawn worker threads (data reception, capture, keepalive, audio)
•
Monitor connection health
•
Detect connection loss via thread exit or socket errors
3.
Cleanup: Stop threads, close socket, reset terminal
4.
Reconnect: Exponential backoff, retry (unless fatal auth error)
5.
Exit: On user signal (SIGINT) or fatal error

Connection Failure Handling

Transient Errors (retryable):

  • Connection refused, timeout, DNS failure → exponential backoff retry
  • Network temporarily unavailable → wait and retry

Fatal Errors (no retry):

  • Authentication failed (CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED) → exit immediately
  • Host key verification failed (CONNECTION_ERROR_HOST_KEY_FAILED) → exit immediately

Thread Management

The client spawns several worker threads coordinated by the main thread:

  • Data Reception Thread: Receives and processes server packets
  • Webcam Capture Thread: Captures frames, transmits to server
  • Ping/Keepalive Thread: Sends periodic pings, detects timeouts
  • Audio Threads (optional): Captures/plays audio with Opus encoding

All threads check the global g_should_exit flag and exit cleanly on shutdown. Main thread waits for all threads to exit before reconnection or final cleanup.

Options

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher (src/main.c) before this function is called. They are available via the global options API (GET_OPTION, etc.).

Key options affecting behavior:

  • opt_address: Server address or session string
  • opt_port: Server port (default 27224)
  • opt_audio_enabled: Enable audio capture/playback
  • opt_snapshot: Capture single frame and exit
  • opt_client_key, opt_server_key: SSH keys for authentication

Returns

Exit code:
  • 0: Success (clean exit or snapshot completed)
  • 1: Initialization/configuration error
  • 2: Connection fatal error (auth failed)
  • 130: Signal interrupt (SIGINT/Ctrl+C)

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat client --address localhost --audio
# Dispatcher (src/main.c) handles:
# - Option parsing
# - Log initialization
# - Signal handler setup
# Then calls client_main() which handles:
# - Subsystem init (display, capture, audio)
# - Connection loop with reconnection logic
# - Thread coordination
# - Graceful shutdown

See also

topic_client "Client Architecture Overview"

topic_client_main "Client Lifecycle Details"

topic_client_connection "Connection Management"

topic_client_protocol "Packet Processing"

Definition at line 631 of file client/main.c.

631                       {
632   log_debug("client_main() starting");
633 
634   // Register client crypto callbacks for TCP connection path
635   // The TCP path in lib/network/connection_attempt.c relies on these callbacks
636   // to handle cryptographic operations and key exchange
637   static const app_callbacks_t client_crypto_callbacks = {
638       .should_exit = should_exit,
639       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
640       .server_connection_set_ip = server_connection_set_ip,
641       .server_connection_set_port = server_connection_set_port,
642       .client_crypto_set_mode = client_crypto_set_mode,
643       .client_crypto_init = client_crypto_init,
644       .client_crypto_handshake = client_crypto_handshake,
645       .crypto_client_is_ready = crypto_client_is_ready,
646       .crypto_client_get_context = crypto_client_get_context,
647   };
648   app_callbacks_register(&client_crypto_callbacks);
649 
650   // Initialize client-specific systems (NOT shared with session_client_like)
651   // This includes: thread pool, display layer, app client context, server connection
652   int init_result = initialize_client_systems();
653   if (init_result != 0) {
654 #ifndef NDEBUG
655     // Debug builds: automatically fall back to test pattern if webcam is in use
656     if (init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE && !GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
657       log_warn("Webcam is in use - automatically falling back to test pattern mode (debug build only)");
658 
659       // Enable test pattern mode via RCU update
660       asciichat_error_t update_result = options_set_bool("test_pattern", true);
661       if (update_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
662         log_error("Failed to update options for test pattern fallback");
663         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
664       }
665 
666       // Retry initialization with test pattern enabled
667       init_result = initialize_client_systems();
668       if (init_result != 0) {
669         log_error("Failed to initialize even with test pattern fallback");
670         webcam_print_init_error_help(init_result);
671         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
672       }
673       log_debug("Successfully initialized with test pattern fallback");
674 
675       // Clear the error state since we successfully recovered
676       CLEAR_ERRNO();
677     } else
678 #endif
679     {
680       // Release builds or other errors: print help and exit
681       if (init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM || init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE || init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION) {
682         webcam_print_init_error_help(init_result);
683         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
684       }
685       // For other errors, just exit with the error code
686       return init_result;
687     }
688   }
689 
690   // Register cleanup function for graceful shutdown
691   (void)atexit(shutdown_client);
692 
693   // Register client interrupt callback (socket shutdown on SIGTERM/Ctrl+C)
694   // Global signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGPIPE, Ctrl+C) are set up in setup_signal_handlers() in src/main.c
695   set_interrupt_callback(server_connection_shutdown);
696 
697 #ifndef _WIN32
698   // Register SIGWINCH for terminal resize handling (client-specific, not in framework)
699   platform_signal(SIGWINCH, client_handle_sigwinch);
700 #endif
701 
702   /* ========================================================================
703    * Client-Specific: LAN/Session Discovery
704    * ========================================================================
705    * This phase discovers the server to connect to via:
706    * - LAN discovery (mDNS)
707    * - Session string lookup (ACDS)
708    * - Direct address/port
709    */
710 
711   // LAN Discovery: If --scan flag is set, discover servers on local network
712   const options_t *opts = options_get();
713   if (opts && opts->lan_discovery &&
714       (opts->address[0] == '\0' || is_localhost_ipv4(opts->address) || strcmp(opts->address, "localhost") == 0)) {
715     log_debug("LAN discovery: --scan flag set, querying for available servers");
716 
717     ui_mdns_config_t lan_config;
718     memset(&lan_config, 0, sizeof(lan_config));
719     lan_config.timeout_ms = 2 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Wait up to 2 seconds for responses
720     lan_config.max_servers = 20;                // Support up to 20 servers on LAN
721     lan_config.quiet = true;                    // Quiet during discovery, TUI will show status
722 
723     int discovered_count = 0;
724     ui_mdns_server_t *discovered_servers = ui_mdns_query(&lan_config, &discovered_count);
725 
726     // Use TUI for server selection
727     int selected_index = ui_mdns_select(discovered_servers, discovered_count);
728 
729     if (selected_index < 0) {
730       // User cancelled or no servers found
731       if (discovered_count == 0) {
732         // No servers found - log message and prevent any further output
733         // Lock the terminal so other threads can't write and our error
734         // will be the last message
735         log_lock_terminal();
736 
737         // Log single message with embedded newlines to prevent multiple log entries
738         log_error("No ascii-chat servers found on the local network.\nUse 'ascii-chat client <address>' to connect "
739                   "manually.");
740 
741         // Exit without cleanup
742         platform_force_exit(1);
743       }
744       // User cancelled (had servers to choose from but pressed cancel)
745       log_debug("LAN discovery: User cancelled server selection");
746       if (discovered_servers) {
747         ui_mdns_free_results(discovered_servers);
748       }
749       return 1; // User cancelled
750     }
751 
752     // Update options with discovered server's address and port
753     ui_mdns_server_t *selected = &discovered_servers[selected_index];
754     const char *selected_address = ui_mdns_get_best_address(selected);
755 
756     // We need to modify options, but they're immutable via RCU
757     // Create a new options copy with updated address/port
758     options_t *opts_new = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(options_t), options_t *);
759     if (opts_new) {
760       memcpy(opts_new, opts, sizeof(options_t));
761       SAFE_STRNCPY(opts_new->address, selected_address, sizeof(opts_new->address));
762 
763       opts_new->port = (int)selected->port;
764 
765       log_debug("LAN discovery: Selected server '%s' at %s:%d", selected->name, opts_new->address, opts_new->port);
766 
767       // Note: In a real scenario, we'd update the global options via RCU
768       // For now, we'll use the updated values directly for connection
769       // This is a known limitation - proper RCU update requires more infrastructure
770     }
771 
772     ui_mdns_free_results(discovered_servers);
773   }
774 
775   // =========================================================================
776   // Client-Specific: Server Address Resolution
777   // =========================================================================
778   // Client mode supports:
779   // 1. Direct address/port (--address HOST --port PORT) - handled by options
780   // 2. LAN discovery (--scan) - handled above
781   // 3. WebSocket URL (direct ws:// or wss:// connection string)
782   //
783   // Note: Session string discovery via ACDS is handled by discovery mode only.
784   //       Client mode does NOT use ACDS or session strings.
785 
786   const char *discovered_address = NULL;
787   int discovered_port = 0;
788 
789   // Check if user provided a WebSocket URL as the server address
790   const options_t *opts_websocket = options_get();
791   const char *provided_address = opts_websocket && opts_websocket->address[0] != '\0' ? opts_websocket->address : NULL;
792 
793   if (provided_address && url_is_websocket(provided_address)) {
794     // Direct WebSocket connection
795     log_debug("Client: Direct WebSocket URL: %s", provided_address);
796     discovered_address = provided_address;
797     discovered_port = 0; // Port is embedded in the URL
798   }
799 
800   if (discovered_port > 0) {
801     log_debug("Client: discovered_address=%s, discovered_port=%d", discovered_address ? discovered_address : "NULL",
802               discovered_port);
803   } else {
804     log_debug("Client: discovered_address=%s", discovered_address ? discovered_address : "NULL");
805   }
806 
807   // Store discovered address/port in session state for client_run() callback
808   static char address_storage[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
809   if (discovered_address) {
810     SAFE_STRNCPY(address_storage, discovered_address, sizeof(address_storage));
811     g_client_session.discovered_address = address_storage;
812     g_client_session.discovered_port = discovered_port;
813   } else {
814     const options_t *opts_fallback = options_get();
815     if (opts_fallback && opts_fallback->address[0] != '\0') {
816       SAFE_STRNCPY(address_storage, opts_fallback->address, sizeof(address_storage));
817       g_client_session.discovered_address = address_storage;
818       g_client_session.discovered_port = opts_fallback->port;
819     } else {
820       g_client_session.discovered_address = "localhost";
821       g_client_session.discovered_port = 27224;
822     }
823   }
824 
825   // Initialize connection context for first attempt
826   asciichat_error_t ctx_init_result = connection_context_init(&g_client_session.connection_ctx);
827   if (ctx_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
828     log_error("Failed to initialize connection context");
829     return 1;
830   }
831 
832   /* ========================================================================
833    * Configure and Run Shared Client-Like Session Framework
834    * ========================================================================
835    * session_client_like_run() handles all shared initialization:
836    * - Terminal output management (force stderr if piped)
837    * - Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
838    * - Splash screen lifecycle
839    * - Media source selection (webcam, file, URL, test pattern)
840    * - FPS probing for media files
841    * - Audio initialization and lifecycle
842    * - Display context creation
843    * - Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)
844    *
845    * Client mode provides:
846    * - client_run() callback: connection loop, protocol startup, monitoring
847    * - client_should_reconnect() callback: reconnection policy
848    * - Reconnection configuration: attempts, delay, callbacks
849    */
850 
851   // Get reconnect attempts setting (-1 = unlimited, 0 = no retry, >0 = retry N times)
852   int reconnect_attempts = GET_OPTION(reconnect_attempts);
853 
854   // Create TCP client for network mode (will be used by session_client_like_run)
855   tcp_client_t *client_tcp = tcp_client_create();
856   if (!client_tcp) {
857     log_error("Failed to create TCP client for connection attempts");
858     return 1; // Return error to exit client
859   }
860 
861   // Configure session_client_like with client-specific settings
862   session_client_like_config_t config = {
863       .run_fn = client_run,
864       .run_user_data = NULL,
865       .tcp_client = client_tcp,
866       .websocket_client = NULL,
867       .discovery = NULL,
868       .custom_should_exit = NULL,
869       .exit_user_data = NULL,
870       .keyboard_handler = NULL, // Client mode: server drives display
871       .max_reconnect_attempts = reconnect_attempts,
872       .should_reconnect_callback = client_should_reconnect,
873       .reconnect_user_data = NULL,
874       .reconnect_delay_ms = 1000,         // 1 second delay between reconnection attempts
875       .print_newline_on_tty_exit = false, // Server/client manages cursor
876   };
877 
878   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call session_client_like_run() with %d attempts", reconnect_attempts);
879   asciichat_error_t session_result = session_client_like_run(&config);
880   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] session_client_like_run() returned %d", session_result);
881 
882   // Note: TCP client lifecycle is managed by session_client_like_run() and connection attempts
883   // Do not destroy it here as it may be reused or already cleaned up
884 
885   // Cleanup connection context
886   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call connection_context_cleanup()");
887   connection_context_cleanup(&g_client_session.connection_ctx);
888   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] connection_context_cleanup() returned");
889 
890   // Cleanup session log buffer (used by splash screen in session_client_like)
891   // NOTE: session_log_buffer_destroy() is already called in session_client_like_run()
892   // during its cleanup phase, so we don't call it here to avoid double-destroy of mutex
893 
894   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] ascii-chat client shutting down");
895 
896   // IMPORTANT: Stop worker threads and join them BEFORE memory report
897   // atexit(shutdown_client) won't run if interrupted by SIGTERM, so call explicitly
898   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call shutdown_client()");
899   fflush(stderr);
900   shutdown_client();
901   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] shutdown_client() returned");
902   fflush(stderr);
903 
904   // Cleanup remaining shared subsystems (buffer pool, platform, etc.)
905   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call asciichat_shared_destroy()");
906   asciichat_shared_destroy();
907 
908   // Log final session statistics before exit
909   // Note: Frame count is logged in protocol_stop_connection() if connection was established
910   // This is a fallback in case the connection never started
911   return (session_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) ? 0 : 1;
912 }




References app_callbacks_register(),
    asciichat_shared_destroy(), client_crypto_handshake(),
    client_crypto_init(), client_crypto_set_mode(),
    connection_context_cleanup(), connection_context_init(),
    crypto_client_get_context(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    is_localhost_ipv4(), log_lock_terminal(),
    options_get(), options_set_bool(),
    server_connection_set_ip(), server_connection_set_port(),
    server_connection_shutdown(), session_client_like_run(),
    set_interrupt_callback(), should_exit(), signal_exit(),
    tcp_client_create(), ui_mdns_free_results(),
    ui_mdns_get_best_address(), ui_mdns_query(),
    ui_mdns_select(), url_is_websocket(), and
    webcam_print_init_error_help().








Variable
  Documentation




app_client_t*
  g_client = NULL


Global client application context. Global application client
    context




Central connection and application state for the client. Contains
    transport-agnostic state: audio, threads, display, crypto. Network-specific
    state (socket, connection flags) is in the active transport client.




Initialized by app_client_create() in client_main().
    Destroyed by app_client_destroy() at cleanup.


Definition at line 152 of file client/main.c.






thread_pool_t*
  g_client_worker_pool = NULL


Global client worker thread pool. Global client worker thread
  pool




Manages all client worker threads including:


    
  
  • Data reception thread (protocol.c)
    • 
  
  • Webcam capture thread (capture.c)
    • 
  
  • Ping/keepalive thread (keepalive.c)
    • 
  
  • Audio capture thread (audio.c)
    • 
  
  • Audio sender thread (audio.c)
    •

Referenced by audio_sender_init(),
    audio_start_thread(), capture_start_thread(),
    keepalive_start_thread(), protocol_start_connection(), and
    protocol_stop_connection().






struct
  webrtc_peer_manager* g_peer_manager = NULL


Global WebRTC peer manager (legacy compatibility). Global WebRTC
    peer manager for P2P connections.




Client mode no longer uses WebRTC, but protocol.c still references
    this. Always NULL in client mode.
